breakdown: Code demonstrates solid architectural separation between model-computed signals and UI formatters with clear naming conventions, but is incomplete (truncated PlanningBlock) and lacks tests—critical for a paradigm shift of this magnitude. Error handling is fail-closed via validation but lacks exception handling with typed errors. Type safety is compromised by Any usage and string literals where enums should be used.
reasoning: Good docstrings and class naming offset by incomplete code, no test file visible, weak error handling (validation returns bool but no explanation of WHY invalid), and type safety gaps (Dict[str, Any], string literals for source). The paradigm shift from XP-threshold inference to model-owned signals is well-documented in comments but implementation appears unfinished.
